posts.author: A_Person_05
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Fiziev vs. Torres
Rafael Fiziev, DEC
People are really hopping on the Torres train again huh? Beating Grant Dawson who has no chin and got knocked out by a jab from Bobby Green, and unmotivated Dober really has people back on the hype train? This guy has never left the first round, he walked into the same punch against Bahamondes like 4 times and got dropped twice. His boxing defense is terrible, his footwork is terrible, he is not particularly fast, or real intelligent in the way he sets up shots, the only thing he has is power. Fiziev can casually outspeed him, move in an out, hit pull counters all night long. Torres will not have power past round 2, and isn't good enough to actually outstrike Fiziev. If Fiziev gets knocked out in round 1 or 2 that sucks, but I do trust him to survive that early onslaught. If you are looking at Fiziev vs Ruffy as evidence that Torres wins this, don't. Torres doesn't have the striking intelligence of Ruffy, or the speed.

Pereira vs. Magomedov
Shara Magomedov, DEC
Pereira somehow gasses out in 30 seconds and then Shara kickboxes his way to a decision, and Shara still doesn't look like anything special by the end of it. It's written in the stars.

Ferreira vs. Aliskerov
Brunno Ferreira, SUB, R2
Ikram doesn't have that it factor. Bruno is way more powerful on the feet, and Ikram's grappling isn't good. I think Bruno just does a casual club and sub, big shot lands, Ikram gets jellylegged, and they go down and Bruno grabs an arm or a choke.

Almabayev vs. Johnson
Charles Johnson, TKO, R3
Almabaev isn't physically strong enough to hold Johnson down, he just isn't. And on the feet he can't compete. Johnson puts a big shot on his chin from a mile away.

Yakhyaev vs. Walker
Abdul Rakhman Yakhyaev, TKO, R1
Cancrusher vs can.

Kuniev vs. Fortune
Rizvan Kuniev, DEC
Kuniev by being mid as opposed to bad.

Ruziboev vs. Pulyaev
Nursulton Ruziboev, TKO, R2
At middleweight Ruziboev seems to be really good. He lands big shots on everyone. At some point he puts a big shot on Pulyaev, and actually follows up with intelligence, unlike Gautier, and gets the TKO.

Almakhan vs. Matsumoto
Jean Matsumoto, DEC
Matsumoto just gets matched up with the toughest unranked dudes in the whole division huh. For the record he beat Rob Font, arguably beat Basharat too. He is such a nice striker, has really nice grappling too. I think Bezkat is too reliant on single shots, and power to beat someone with actual depth of talent to their striking.

Donchenko vs. Gustafsson
Daniil Donchenko, TKO, R1
Gustafsson seemingly has no chin. Rinat knocked him out with a shot that didn't even seem very powerful. It's a shame because Gustafsson has a seemingly sick style, but you can't be a mauler that can't get hit.

Sadykhov vs. Camilo
Nazim Sadykhov, DEC
Sdykhov bounces back in style. Violence is back on the menu boys.
